behaviors.description: Reject invalid HMAC signature with 401 and do not forward body
behaviors.justification: Escalation phrase: "signature does not match ... 401 ... body is not forwarded" requires handler->signature-verifier->queue handoff behavior, not just pure hash math. Lower-level miss: a Unit test of HMAC comparison could pass while the HTTP layer still enqueues on failure due to wiring/branch bug. Rejected higher level with disqualifying text: behavior only specifies status code and forwarding suppression, not real network timing/OS effects, and no AI/model step.
behaviors.test_description: Run the HTTP handler in-process with a fake/stub queue module and real signature-check path; send a request with mismatched signature, assert 401, and assert queue write was never called.

behaviors.description: Persist event to queue before returning HTTP 200
behaviors.justification: Escalation phrase: "written to the persistent queue before the HTTP 200 acknowledgment" is an ordering contract across handler and queue modules. Lower-level miss: Unit-testing only response code can pass even if code sends 200 before awaiting queue persistence call completion. Rejected higher level: text does not require real network/OS semantics beyond module ordering, and no model dependence.
behaviors.test_description: Use in-process handler plus a controllable queue adapter promise; verify 200 is not emitted until queue write resolves, and emitted after successful write.

behaviors.description: Return HTTP response without waiting for downstream processing completion
behaviors.justification: Escalation phrase: "returns the HTTP response without waiting for downstream processing ... to finish" is async decoupling between ingress path and worker path. Lower-level miss: a Unit test of a single function can miss accidental await/join on downstream processor introduced in integration wiring. Rejected higher level: behavior does not require real OS/network conditions, only local async orchestration.
behaviors.test_description: Wire handler with a downstream processor that blocks on a deferred promise; assert response is returned before deferred is released, and processing can finish later.

behaviors.description: Respond within 3 seconds under concurrent slow downstream load
behaviors.justification: Escalation phrase: "within 3 seconds wall-clock time even when downstream processing ... is slow and concurrent" depends on real runtime scheduling, socket handling, and contention under concurrency. Lower-level miss: Integration tests with mocked clocks/fakes can pass while real server/event-loop starvation causes >3s latency. Rejected higher level: no AI/model output and no multi-agentic chain.
behaviors.test_description: Start the real Node HTTP server, generate concurrent requests while workers for earlier events are intentionally slow, and measure end-to-end wall-clock latency of new requests; assert responses stay <3s.

behaviors.description: Do not write duplicate event IDs to queue
behaviors.justification: Escalation phrase: "ID was already received ... not written ... a second time" is dedupe state handoff across request handling and queue/storage boundary. Lower-level miss: Unit logic for ID comparison can pass while persistence uniqueness/upsert wiring still allows duplicate enqueue across requests. Rejected higher level: no requirement for real OS/network timing and no AI dependency.
behaviors.test_description: Issue two in-process handler calls with same event ID against a real in-memory/local persistence-backed queue module; assert only one queue record/write is committed.

behaviors.description: Return 503 + Retry-After when queue is full
behaviors.justification: Escalation phrase: "When the queue reports it is full, handler returns 503 with Retry-After" is translation of queue-domain error to HTTP contract across module boundary. Lower-level miss: Unit-testing header formatting alone can pass while actual error mapping middleware drops/rewrites status/header. Rejected higher level: this behavior does not require proving real capacity exhaustion mechanics (covered by behavior 7), only mapping of a reported condition.
behaviors.test_description: Configure queue module to return/throw a queue-full condition; call handler in-process and assert HTTP 503 plus correct Retry-After header.

behaviors.description: Persistent queue enforces configured capacity and rejects overflow
behaviors.justification: Escalation phrase: "persistent queue actually enforces its configured capacity limit" targets queue + persistence implementation interaction, not just handler branching. Lower-level miss: Unit tests with mocked storage can pass while real queue repository logic ignores configured limit during actual writes. Rejected higher level: behavior does not require real external network/subprocess; local persistence module wiring is sufficient, and no AI/model output involved.
behaviors.test_description: Instantiate the real queue component with local persistence backend and small capacity, write up to limit, then attempt one more write and assert deterministic rejection/error.
